query: effectiveness of avelumab in treating bladder cancer

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Medical Oncology -- Metastatic Urothelial Disease
retrieved_docs.content: Paclitaxel binds to tubulin and halts intracellular microtubule production, which blocks cell mitosis. [285] [286] This therapy has demonstrated a very good response rate, with 27% of patients exhibiting a complete response. [285] [287] Immune checkpoint inhibitors have shown activity in metastatic urothelial malignancies. These drugs reduce natural T-cell inhibition from their programmed death protein-1 (PD-1) cell surface protein, essentially releasing the T-cell's full immune potential. Examples include pembrolizumab, atezolizumab, avelumab, and nivolumab, which have all been approved for use in bladder cancer patients. Avelumab and pembrolizumab have shown improved overall survival in clinical trials. [288] [289] [290] [291]

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Enfortumab vedotin and pembrolizumab (EV+P) have replaced cisplatin-based treatments as first-line chemotherapy for advanced or metastatic urothelial (bladder) cancer. [297] [298] [299]

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Medical Oncology -- Metastatic Urothelial Disease
retrieved_docs.content: The latest recommendation from the FDA and the European Society of Medical Oncology is to use a combination of enfortumab vedotin and pembrolizumab (EV+P) as first-line chemotherapy for advanced or metastatic urothelial (bladder) cancer. [288] [297]

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Medical Oncology -- Metastatic Urothelial Disease
retrieved_docs.content: Erdafitinib is a tyrosine kinase inhibitor, which acts as a fibroblast growth factor blocker that is indicated for metastatic urothelial cancer patients who progressed on cisplatin-based chemotherapy. Hyperphosphatemia is common, and up to 25% of patients will need to stop therapy due to significant retinal eye issues. [292] [293] [294] [295] Enfortumab vedotin is an antibody-drug conjugate, where a monoclonal antibody is attached to an anticancer drug. [296] [297] Pembrolizumab is an immune checkpoint (PD-1) inhibitor. Results from a large, multinational trial involving 185 centers in 25 different countries recently demonstrated that combination therapy with enfortumab vedotin and pembrolizumab (EF+P) in advanced urothelial cancer patients nearly doubled their progression-free and overall survival. [298] [299]

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Treatment / Management -- Therapy for BCG failures
retrieved_docs.content: An abstract from the 2023 European Society of Medical Oncology (ESMO) by Necchi and associates described their early results with a unique investigational implantable drug delivery device (TAR-200) allowing for the sustained delivery of intravesical chemotherapy to the bladder for patients with high-risk BCG-unresponsive CIS. This intravesical device using gemcitabine has shown a complete response rate of 77%, of which over 90% have been maintained over a median of 48 weeks. It is now being tested in combination with cetrelimab, an investigational programmed cell death receptor-1 (PD-1) monoclonal antibody. Further studies are needed to determine and verify the duration and optimal usage of such therapies.

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: Intravesical nogapendekin alfa inbakicept plus BCG therapy is now approved for high-risk BCG-unresponsive patients with CIS with or without Ta or T1 disease. [220]

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Treatment / Management -- Therapy for BCG failures
retrieved_docs.content: Other investigational therapies include immune checkpoint inhibitors, oncolytic virus regimens, recombinant fusion proteins, immune modulation therapies, cytotoxic agents (cabazitaxel, cisplatinum, and gemcitabine,) and targeted small molecule kinase inhibitors. [36] [223]

retrieved_docs.title: Atezolizumab -- Indications -- Locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma
retrieved_docs.content: Atezolizumab monotherapy is indicated for patients with locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma who: Are ineligible for cisplatin-containing chemotherapy with a level of tumor PD-L1 expression, or Are ineligible for any platinum-containing chemotherapy irrespective of their PD-L1expression status, or Exhibit disease progression during or following any platinum-containing chemotherapy or within 12 months of neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy [7] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Atezolizumab -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Atezolizumab is a humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody that targets programmed cell death ligand 1 (PD-L1). Atezolizumab has received FDA approval for multiple neoplastic conditions and may be administered as monotherapy or in combination with chemotherapy. Indications include locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma, metastatic non-small cell lung cancer, extensive-stage small cell lung cancer, metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, and unresectable or metastatic hepatocellular carcinoma.

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Treatment / Management -- MUSCLE-INVASIVE BLADDER CANCER
retrieved_docs.content: Adjuvant chemotherapy is given after surgery and reserved for patients with a high recurrence risk. This would include patients with extravesical disease and those with positive lymph nodes at the time of surgery who did not receive preoperative chemotherapy. Nivolumab, an immune checkpoint inhibitor, is also FDA-approved for adjuvant therapy in patients with urothelial bladder cancer at high risk for a recurrence who refused, could not tolerate, or were ineligible for prior chemotherapy. [263] [264] Unlike neoadjuvant chemotherapy, there is no definitive evidence of any significant benefit to adjuvant chemotherapy. However, adjuvant radiation therapy may help with local control and disease-specific survival but has not demonstrated any overall survival benefit when used alone. Fiduciary markers can help target radiation therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Treatment / Management -- Therapy for BCG failures
retrieved_docs.content: Pembrolizumab, an alternative IV immunotherapy, may be considered for patients with recurrent CIS that returns within a year of completing BCG therapy. [36]

retrieved_docs.title: Pembrolizumab -- Indications -- Urothelial Carcinoma
retrieved_docs.content: Locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma who have progressed on platinum-based chemotherapy Locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma within 12 months of neoadjuvant/adjuvant platinum-based chemotherapy. In patients with previously treated advanced urothelial cancer, treatment with pembrolizumab resulted in a median one-year survival rate of 44.2% compared to 29.8% for chemotherapy (paclitaxel or docetaxel or vinflunine) alone. The median two-year survival rate was 26.9% for pembrolizumab compared to 11.0% for chemotherapy alone (at a median follow-up of 27.7 months). [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Pembrolizumab -- Indications -- Urothelial Carcinoma
retrieved_docs.content: Locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma with tumor expression PD-L1 of greater than 10%, ineligible for cisplatin-containing chemotherapy. In patients with advanced urothelial carcinoma who were ineligible for cisplatin, treatment with pembrolizumab resulted in a 29% objective response rate and a median follow-up of 7.8 months. [14] Locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma not eligible for any platinum-based chemotherapy

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Treatment / Management -- Therapy for BCG failures
retrieved_docs.content: Cretostimogene is a unique investigational oncolytic immunotherapy that has produced durable complete response rates of more than 75% lasting over a year in patients with BCG-unresponsive CIS. These preliminary results are quite encouraging.

retrieved_docs.title: Bladder Cancer -- Pearls and Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: A persistently positive FISH test after BCG induction therapy is a poor prognostic indicator. [36]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 1948
retrieved_docs.content: Chlorambucil, procarbazine, vincristine sulfate, brentuximab vedotin, cyclophosphamide anhydrous, doxorubicin hydrochloride, ifosfamide, pembrolizumab, streptozocin, vinblastine sulfate, and vinorelbine tartrate are all agents that may be used to treat مرض هودجكين (Hodgkin disease). These drugs represent a range of therapeutic classes, including alkylating agents (chlorambucil, cyclophosphamide anhydrous, ifosfamide, procarbazine), anthracycline chemotherapy (doxorubicin hydrochloride), vinca‑alkaloid compounds (vincristine sulfate, vinblastine sulfate, vinorelbine tartrate), a nitrosourea derivative (streptozocin), a monoclonal antibody‑drug conjugate targeting CD30 (brentuximab vedotin), and an immune checkpoint inhibitor that blocks PD‑1 (pembrolizumab). The inclusion of both traditional cytotoxic chemotherapies and newer targeted or immunotherapeutic agents reflects the evolving multimodal approach to managing Hodgkin disease, aiming to improve response rates while balancing toxicity. Effective patient care involves selecting appropriate agents based on disease stage, prior treatment exposure, and individual patient factors, often combining several of these medications within established chemotherapy regimens or as part of salvage and maintenance strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 327
retrieved_docs.content: Canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab are both classified as epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors. As members of the EGFR inhibitor class, these agents act on the EGFR signaling pathway, which plays a critical role in the growth and survival of certain cancer cells. Recognizing that both canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab share this mechanism of action helps clinicians select appropriate targeted therapies and anticipate similar pharmacologic effects and potential adverse events associated with EGFR inhibition.

Pembrolizumab is a programmed death‑1 (PD‑1) immune checkpoint inhibitor that enhances antitumor immunity in a wide range of cancers, including melanoma, non‑small cell lung cancer, and urothelial carcinoma. Sargramostim is a recombinant granulocyte‑macrophage colony‑stimulating factor (GM‑CSF) used to accelerate recovery of white blood cells after chemotherapy or bone‑marrow transplantation. EGFR inhibitors are a subset of protein kinase inhibitors that possess antineoplastic and immunomodulating properties, placing them within the broader therapeutic category of agents used to treat cancer and modulate immune responses.

Targeted and immunomodulatory therapies are highlighted by brentuximab vedotin (a monoclonal antibody‑drug conjugate), pembrolizumab (a PD‑1 checkpoint inhibitor) and sargramostim (a recombinant granulocyte‑macrophage colony‑stimulating factor), each directing immune mechanisms against Hodgkin lymphoma cells. Collectively, these relationships illustrate a multimodal treatment paradigm that integrates alkylators, platinum compounds, anthracyclines, antimitotics, antibiotics, monoclonal antibodies and immune‑stimulating agents—used alone or in combination—to manage Hodgkin disease, underscoring the importance of diverse chemotherapy regimens and emerging immunotherapies in contemporary patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 531
retrieved_docs.content: Conventional alkylating and antimetabolite chemotherapies—chlorambucil, cyclophosphamide anhydrous, ifosfamide, mechlorethamine, procarbazine (including its hydrochloride form), dakarbazin and lomustine—exert cytotoxic effects on rapidly dividing Hodgkin lymphoma cells. Platinum‑based therapy with cisplatin and anthracycline antibiotics doxorubicin hydrochloride (both standard and liposomal formulations) add DNA‑damaging mechanisms. Additional cytotoxic agents used in Hodgkin protocols include bleomycin sulfate, vinblastine sulfate, vinorelbine tartrate and streptozocin, each targeting distinct aspects of tumor proliferation and survival. Targeted and immunomodulatory drugs expand therapeutic options: brentuximab vedotin delivers an anti‑CD30 antibody‑drug conjugate directly to Hodgkin lymphoma cells; pembrolizumab, a PD‑1 checkpoint inhibitor, enhances antitumor immune responses; and sargramostim, a recombinant granulocyte‑macrophage colony‑stimulating factor, can augment host

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1281
retrieved_docs.content: Chlorambucil, dakarbazin, bleomycin sulfate, chlormethin hydrochloride, cyclophosphamide anhydrous, doxorubicin hydrochloride, liposomal doxorubicin hydrochloride, ifosfamide, pembrolizumab, streptozocin, and vinorelbine tartrate are all identified as agents that may be used to treat Hodgkin disease (مرض هودجكين). These drugs represent a range of therapeutic classes, including alkylating agents (chlorambucil, cyclophosphamide anhydrous, ifosfamide), a bleomycin formulation, anthracyclines (doxorubicin hydrochloride and its liposomal form), a nucleoside analog (streptozocin), a vinca‑alkaloid derivative (vinorelbine tartrate), a pyrimidine analog (chlormethin hydrochloride), and an immune checkpoint inhibitor (pembrolizumab). The inclusion of both conventional cytotoxic chemotherapy agents and newer targeted immunotherapy reflects the multifaceted approach to managing Hodgkin disease, where combination regimens are often employed to improve response rates and reduce relapse. Recognizing the diverse pharmacologic options available for Hodgkin disease supports personalized treatment planning and informs clinicians about potential alternatives when standard therapies are contraindicated or ineffective.
